bins.name: Clear interconnection rules and joint studies
bins.desc: Enchanted Rock supports clear, predictable federal transmission interconnection rules with joint study processes that evaluate co-located and hybrid facilities based on net grid impacts, ensuring legal durability and alignment with NERC reliability standards.
quotes: Enchanted Rock generally supports the Secretary of Energy's position regarding the need to ensure "access to affordable, reliable, and secure electricity" by facilitating the connection of large loads "to the transmission system in a timely, orderly, and non-discriminatory manner."

Winning the AI race and bolstering the U.S. economy relies on the ability of federal and state regulators to create stable and predictable rules that encourage infrastructure investment and business confidence.

Vistra's comments align with Enchanted Rock's initial comments seeking further clarification on the dividing line between federal and state regulation of large load interconnection, and demonstrate the need for the Commission to promulgate a final rule that is clear, predictable and legally durable.

Enchanted Rock acknowledges CAISO's comments that large loads connecting to the transmission system pose significant impacts on system reliability, and supports efforts to harmonize NERC's reliability standards with the reforms being considered in this ANOPR proceeding.

A joint study process would allow LCDG and hybrid facilities to be studied based on their operational behavior and net load impacts, which Enchanted Rock believes will result in less strain on the transmission system, unlock available grid headroom, optimize existing transmission infrastructure and more accurately model the flexible capabilities of the system.

Enchanted Rock encourages the Commission to adopt rules that will facilitate joint study processes (even if only for a provisional period) and that evaluate co-located and hybrid facilities based on their net impacts to the grid.

Enchanted Rock urges the Commission to prioritize the development of clear transmission interconnection rules and consider the value of flexibility in accelerating large load interconnections, while protecting ratepayer affordability and grid reliability.

bins.name: Load flexibility for speed-to-power
bins.desc: The filer advocates flexible load models as essential to achieving rapid power delivery while managing high volumes of large load interconnections, enabling interruptible service, co-located generation integration, and more accurate system modeling of flexible capabilities.
quotes: These Reply Comments reiterate the urgent need to facilitate load flexibility as a mechanism to accomplish speed-to-power, which will reinforce grid reliability, support energy affordability, and offer a technology-neutral pathway to secure necessary generation.

Acknowledging load flexibility during the transmission planning and interconnection processes allows transmission planners to assume lower peak demand for the associated load facility, thereby reducing the immediate need for transmission upgrades and expediting interconnection timelines.

Transmission system operators and planners urgently require a solution capable of efficiently managing the high volume of large load interconnections and accommodating the aggregate demand introduced by these facilities.

Google recommends that the Commission, in the near term, prioritize "facilitating the integration of co-located generation, including electric storage resources, and load and electrically proximate load and generation, which unlock planning and siting efficiencies that minimize transmission impact."

A joint study process would allow LCDG and hybrid facilities to be studied based on their operational behavior and net load impacts, which Enchanted Rock believes will result in less strain on the transmission system, unlock available grid headroom, optimize existing transmission infrastructure and more accurately model the flexible capabilities of the system.
Flexible models, such as LCDG, offer a practical approach to meet accelerating energy "without driving up costs or abandoning progress on efficiency, resilience and carbon reduction."
if the customer chooses, interruptible service can serve as a bridge to full service while transmission upgrades are completed

Enchanted Rock urges the Commission to prioritize the development of clear transmission interconnection rules and consider the value of flexibility in accelerating large load interconnections, while protecting ratepayer affordability and grid reliability.

bins.name: Transmission cost reduction through flexibility and co-location
bins.desc: Enchanted Rock argues that acknowledging load flexibility and co-locating generation with load reduces peak demand assumptions, minimizes transmission upgrades, and mitigates costs passed to ratepayers while supporting affordability.
quotes: Acknowledging load flexibility during the transmission planning and interconnection processes allows transmission planners to assume lower peak demand for the associated load facility, thereby reducing the immediate need for transmission upgrades and expediting interconnection timelines.
Flexible models, such as LCDG, offer a practical approach to meet accelerating energy "without driving up costs or abandoning progress on efficiency, resilience and carbon reduction."

Co-locating generation with load allows system planners to study the assets as a combined facility, triggering fewer transmission upgrades and mitigating the associated costs that would otherwise be passed onto rate payers.

Enchanted Rock urges the Commission to prioritize the development of clear transmission interconnection rules and consider the value of flexibility in accelerating large load interconnections, while protecting ratepayer affordability and grid reliability.

bins.name: Co-located generation and hybrid resource integration
bins.desc: The filer supports co-location of generation with large loads and hybrid facilities to optimize transmission infrastructure, reduce planning burdens, trigger fewer upgrades, and unlock grid headroom through net-impact evaluation.
quotes: Google recommends that the Commission, in the near term, prioritize "facilitating the integration of co-located generation, including electric storage resources, and load and electrically proximate load and generation, which unlock planning and siting efficiencies that minimize transmission impact."

Enchanted Rock encourages the Commission to adopt rules that will facilitate joint study processes (even if only for a provisional period) and that evaluate co-located and hybrid facilities based on their net impacts to the grid.

Co-locating generation with load allows system planners to study the assets as a combined facility, triggering fewer transmission upgrades and mitigating the associated costs that would otherwise be passed onto rate payers.
